title: Triumph.
content:
Triumph may be of several kinds.
    There 's triumph in the room
    When that old imperator, Death,
    By faith is overcome.

    There 's triumph of the finer mind
    When truth, affronted long,
    Advances calm to her supreme,
    Her God her only throng.

    A triumph when temptation's bribe
    Is slowly handed back,
    One eye upon the heaven renounced
    And one upon the rack.

    Severer triumph, by himself
    Experienced, who can pass
    Acquitted from that naked bar,
    Jehovah's countenance!
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Triumph."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
